description: You are a Senior BI Analyst at an e-commerce retailer. Using the CLICK_ANALYST schema in Oracle (Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1, User=click_analyst, Password=Click2024), process raw clickstream data from the EVENTS table into actionable user journeys and funnel analytics. Sessionize events into a new table CLICK_ANALYST.USER_SESSIONS (session_id, user_id, session_start, session_end, event_count, session_duration_secs) where a new session begins after a gap of >30 minutes between consecutive events for a user. Detect funnel patterns in CLICK_ANALYST.FUNNEL_PATTERNS (pattern_id, user_id, session_id, pattern_type), identifying 'COMPLETE_CONVERSION' (the sequence: page_view -> product_view -> add_to_cart -> checkout_start -> purchase), 'CART_ABANDONMENT', and 'CHECKOUT_ABANDONMENT'. Populate CLICK_ANALYST.USER_SEGMENTS (user_id, segment_name, total_sessions, total_purchases) categorizing users as 'ONE_TIME_VISITOR' (1 session, 0 purchases), 'RETURNING_BROWSER' (>1 session, 0 purchases), or 'CUSTOMER' (>0 purchases). Create a view named CONVERSION_FUNNEL_VW showing stage-by-stage drop-off metrics and a materialized view named ENGAGEMENT_DASHBOARD_MV showing daily sessions, unique users, and a 7-day moving average of sessions. Export the conversion funnel report to /home/ga/funnel_report.csv.
